prior_reviews: W1 (Modal/URL drift — fixed by baseline notes)

evidence: src/elspeth/web/sessions/routes.py:1297 — transcript_url: None confirmed (plan acknowledges this correctly)
src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py:2977-3117 — failed_turn FailedTurnMetadata wired across multiple exception sites
phase_3_contracts_verified.notes: All Phase 3 contracts assumed by Phase 4 are live. Backend dependency is clean.

run_one_turn_for_test_callers.risk: The integration eval characterization test at line 262 calls driver._run_one_turn_for_test() via cast(Any, service). It is not in the Task 0 Step 5 focused verification run. Method-name preservation makes the post-refactor test likely green, but the cleanup verification does not observe it. A regression here would only surface if the integration suite runs separately.

api_error_consumer_ripple.analysis: All consumers use the `as ApiError` cast pattern or check specific known fields. Adding optional partial_state / failed_turn / partial_state_save_failed / partial_state_save_error fields to the interface is purely additive. No existing consumer will break.

second_order_effects.title: Recovery state leaks across sessions — missing session-switch reset sites
second_order_effects.description: Task 3 Step 2 adds recoveryError and recoveryStartedEditorRevision to sessionStore state but the plan does not say to add these fields to any of the four session-switch reset sites.

session_switch_sites_with_explicit_reset: sessionStore.ts:175 — createSession: resets compositionState, selectedNodeId, error, clearedGuidedState()
sessionStore.ts:211 — archiveSession active-session branch: resets compositionState, selectedNodeId, clearedGuidedState()
sessionStore.ts:237 — selectSession: resets compositionState, selectedNodeId, error, clearedGuidedState()
sessionStore.ts:547 — forkFromMessage: resets compositionState, selectedNodeId, clearedGuidedState()
evidence.corollary: A user who opens the recovery panel on session A, then switches to session B without dismissing, will carry recoveryError into session B. The panel will render session A's failed_turn and partial_state on top of session B's editor state. Applying recovery in session B would install session A's composition state into session B.

second_order_effects.recommendation: Task 3 Step 2 must explicitly list these reset sites and add the new fields to each. The clearedGuidedState() helper pattern (introduced at lines 89-99 to avoid drift) should be replicated: extract a clearedRecoveryState() helper and compose it into all four sites.

second_order_effects.title: editorRevision increment trigger is undefined — concurrent-edit guard correctness is unspecified
second_order_effects.description: Task 3 Step 2 introduces editorRevision (or equivalent edit counter) as a store field. The concurrent-edit guard compares a captured revision at compose-start against the current revision at Apply time. The plan never specifies what increments editorRevision.

evidence.grep_result: No match for editorRevision, editCounter, edit_counter, compositionRevision anywhere in src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/
compositionState_mutation_paths: sessionStore.ts:328 — sendMessage success path sets compositionState
sessionStore.ts:483 — retryMessage success path sets compositionState
sessionStore.ts:551 — forkFromMessage success path sets compositionState
evidence.missing: No path increments an edit counter on local (user-driven) edits to nodes, edges, source, or outputs. The plan says 'compare a captured editor revision/edit counter, not object identity of the state payload' (Task 2 Step 1) but never says where that counter is incremented in the composer's node-edit flow.

second_order_effects.risk: If editorRevision only increments on successful compose turns (server-side state updates), the guard misses local user edits made directly in the node inspector/editor after the compose started but before it failed. The user edits their state, the compose fails, they open the panel, the guard sees no revision change (both sides are the last server-confirmed revision), and the panel applies without a confirmation — silently overwriting the user's local edits.
second_order_effects.recommendation: The plan must specify every code path that mutates compositionState in the UI (node add/remove/edit, edge add/remove, source change, output change) and which of those increment editorRevision. If the composer editor doesn't yet have local-edit tracking, Task 3 must add it. The spec §7.2 concurrent-edit guard claim needs a mechanically verified answer.

second_order_effects.title: Recovery panel re-renders trigger repeated audit_grade_view records
second_order_effects.description: RecoveryTranscript (Task 5) calls fetchRecoveryTranscript(sessionId, { limit: 500 }). This hits GET /api/sessions/{sid}/messages?include_tool_rows=true. The route handler at sessions/routes.py:4173 unconditionally calls record_audit_grade_view_async for every include_tool_rows=true request. If RecoveryTranscript uses a standard useEffect without an explicit fetch-once guard (e.g., fetches on every parent re-render), each panel open triggers N audit_access_log rows.

evidence.route_code: src/elspeth/web/sessions/routes.py:4173-4181 — record_audit_grade_view_async fires on every request with include_tool_rows=true
evidence.spec_counter: spec §8.3.2 — composer.audit.audit_grade_view_total is an OTel counter with no SLO cap
evidence.plan_gap: Task 5 Step 2 does not specify a fetch-once pattern or state guard

second_order_effects.recommendation: Task 5 Step 1 tests should assert that fetchRecoveryTranscript is called exactly once per panel open, not once per render cycle. Task 5 Step 2 should specify useEffect with a stable dependency array (e.g., [sessionId, failedTurnAssistantMessageId]) to prevent re-fetch on unrelated re-renders.

second_order_effects.title: Retry-after-failure opens a second recovery panel without defined behavior
second_order_effects.description: The plan says 'open the panel from the existing sendMessage / retryMessage catch paths' (Current Code Baseline). If the user sees the recovery panel, dismisses it without applying (Discard), then retries the same message (retryMessage), and the retry also fails with partial_state + failed_turn, openRecoveryFromError fires again. The plan does not specify whether the second open replaces the first partial_state (the second attempt's intermediate state) or appends, and whether a Discard of the first recovery is a prerequisite.

evidence.retryMessage_catch: sessionStore.ts:502-526 — catch block calls openRecoveryFromError (per Task 3 plan), which would overwrite recoveryError
evidence.plan_gap: No mention of what happens to the first panel's state when a second error arrives

second_order_effects.recommendation: Task 3 Step 1 should add a test case: retry after panel open replaces recoveryError with the newer error object. Task 3 Step 2 should make this replacement semantics explicit in the action. Confirm discardRecovery is idempotent (calling it when already null is a no-op).

second_order_effects.title: z-index stacking order unspecified — recovery panel may render below existing dialogs
second_order_effects.description: The App.tsx renders ConfirmDialog (fanout guard) at --z-dialog (201) and CommandPalette at --z-palette (301). Task 7 renders the RecoveryPanel 'near App.' Task 6 says 'use existing local dialog patterns.' If the implementer chooses --z-dialog (201) for the recovery panel, a simultaneously-open fanout ConfirmDialog will collide at the same stacking level. If they choose --z-overlay (100), the recovery panel renders below both ConfirmDialog and CommandPalette.

evidence.existing_fanout_dialog_site: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/App.tsx:252-261 — pendingFanoutGuard ConfirmDialog rendered at App level without explicit z-index class (inherits --z-dialog via .confirm-dialog CSS rule at App.css:939)

second_order_effects.recommendation: Task 6 should explicitly assign --z-dialog (201) to the recovery panel backdrop/panel pair. If two dialogs at the same z-index can be open simultaneously (fanout guard + recovery panel), add a note that later-rendered elements win CSS stacking within the same z-value, which may be sufficient but should be tested. Task 8 Step 2 smoke test item 1 should include simultaneous fanout-guard + recovery-panel visibility.

second_order_effects.title: applyRecoveredState succeeds in UI but parallel save could leave state mismatched
second_order_effects.description: Task 3 Step 2 states applyRecoveredState() 'replaces compositionState, clears stale validation through the execution store, clears selected node when necessary, and closes the panel.' This is a local UI-only state update. The partial_state that was already persisted to the DB by the Phase 3 backend is a separate DB row. If applyRecoveredState sets compositionState locally but the user immediately closes the browser (or network drops), the local compositionState differs from the server's authoritative compositionState until the next mutation triggers a server sync.

evidence.sendMessage_pattern: sessionStore.ts:301-331 — compositionState is only server-authoritative after sendMessage/retryMessage/revertToVersion succeed; local state between those calls is optimistic
evidence.apply_recovery_gap: Plan does not specify whether applyRecoveredState triggers a server-side state promotion or remains purely local until the next user compose action

second_order_effects.recommendation: Clarify in Task 3 Step 2 whether applyRecoveredState is purely local (compositionState reflects partial_state until next send) or whether it triggers a PATCH/PUT to the server to set the partial_state as the active composition. The spec §7 should be consulted for the intended contract. Either answer is acceptable but the plan should state it explicitly so the test in Task 3 Step 1 asserts the correct behavior.

second_order_effects.title: Missing schema-version guard on partial_state deserialization after mid-session deploy
second_order_effects.description: CompositionState in the frontend (types/index.ts:157) has a row version field (integer, from the DB) but no protocol/schema version. If the backend is deployed mid-session (new fields added to CompositionState serialization), the partial_state stored before the deploy may not match the TypeScript type expected after the deploy. applyRecoveredState would install an incomplete object into compositionState.

evidence.compositionstate_type: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts:157-168 — version is row version (int), no schema_version field
evidence.partial_state_shape: src/elspeth/web/sessions/routes.py:1397-1398 — partial_state is serialized via exc.partial_state.to_dict() then redact_source_storage_path()

second_order_effects.recommendation: Task 1 Step 2 should note this is a known gap. The immediate risk is low (no deploy between persist and recovery within a single session is the common case), but the plan should document that the 'Apply' button on stale partial_state from a prior backend version is not validated for shape compatibility.

loops.description: RecoveryTranscript re-render -> fetch -> audit_access_log write -> (if error) error state -> re-render
loops.risk: Low if useEffect is correctly gated; moderate if fetch-on-every-render

loops.description: User retries -> failure -> recovery panel opens -> user discards -> user retries -> failure -> recovery panel opens
loops.risk: Low systemic risk; UX concern. Second recovery panel open replaces the first, which is acceptable.
loops.mitigation_in_plan: Partially (plan covers Discard behavior) but does not specify the second-open replacement semantics explicitly

historical_pattern_matches.pattern: Implicit state leak across context switch
historical_pattern_matches.concern: Recovery state fields added to a store that has multiple explicit reset sites. Each new field requires updating each reset site. The clearedGuidedState() helper exists precisely because this pattern recurred with guided fields (comment at sessionStore.ts:82-88). The plan adds new fields without following the same discipline.

historical_pattern_matches.pattern: Optimistic UI with undefined server-sync contract
historical_pattern_matches.concern: applyRecoveredState is specified as a local UI action. Whether it creates a server record of 'user chose this partial_state as their new active state' is unspecified.

historical_pattern_matches.pattern: Big bang (4A cleanup + 4B feature in one PR)
historical_pattern_matches.concern: Task 0 (Python refactor) + Tasks 1-9 (TypeScript feature) are one PR. Reviewers must context-switch between a 4247-line Python service refactor and a multi-component React feature. The plan is aware of this (PR description requirements in Task 9 Step 4) but does not offer the Task 0 split-as-prerequisite option.
historical_pattern_matches.plan_reference: Overview notes Phase 4A as cleanup before Phase 4B

failure_mode_analysis.silent_failure_impact: _phase3_last_tool_outcomes and similar fields not initialized on every path; first access raises AttributeError mid-compose turn. Audit trail records a Tier-1 crash.
failure_mode_analysis.loud_failure_impact: pytest immediately fails on test_compose_loop_persistence.py or test_audit_failure_primacy.py
failure_mode_analysis.mitigation_in_plan: Step 5 focused test run catches behavioral drift

failure_mode_analysis.change: Task 1 — ApiError widening with recovery fields
failure_mode_analysis.silent_failure_impact: parseResponse catches body.partial_state but assigns to undefined field name -> TypeScript error at compile time
failure_mode_analysis.loud_failure_impact: TypeScript check in Task 9 Step 2 fails

failure_mode_analysis.silent_failure_impact: If called with non-recovery ApiError (e.g. 502 llm_unavailable), spec says the panel stays closed. If the guard condition in openRecoveryFromError is wrong, the panel opens for non-recovery errors with null partial_state/failed_turn — rendering an empty or broken panel silently.
failure_mode_analysis.loud_failure_impact: RecoveryDiff receives null compositionState and throws, surfacing an error boundary
failure_mode_analysis.mitigation_in_plan: Task 3 Step 1 specifies the '422 convergence error without recovery fields does not open recovery state' test case — adequate

failure_mode_analysis.silent_failure_impact: If the session has >500 combined assistant+tool rows, paging at offset=0 limit=500 returns only the first 500. The specific assistant row for failed_turn.assistant_message_id may be beyond that window. filterByfailed_turn.assistant_message_id returns empty array. Panel shows degraded 'transcript unavailable' state without error.
failure_mode_analysis.loud_failure_impact: None — plan already handles null assistant_message_id as degraded diagnostic
failure_mode_analysis.mitigation_in_plan: Partially — plan says 'handles failed_turn.assistant_message_id === null by showing documented degraded diagnostic' but does not cover the case where the message ID is non-null but beyond the 500-row window

failure_mode_analysis.change: Task 7 — render recovery panel near App
failure_mode_analysis.silent_failure_impact: Panel mounts with stale activeSessionId if the user switches sessions while the panel is open — can apply session A's state to session B
failure_mode_analysis.mitigation_in_plan: No stale-session guard mentioned for Task 7 (mirrors the selectSession stale-fetch guard pattern at line 592 but for a panel, not a fetch)

timing_assumptions.what_breaks_it: fetchRecoveryTranscript(limit=500) is a round-trip fetch that happens BEFORE transcript rows can be rendered. On a slow connection or a session with many rows (server fetches ALL messages then slices: routes.py:4187), the combined fetch + diff computation + React render could exceed 500ms for non-trivial sessions.
timing_assumptions.notes: The target is a 'sanity target' not an SLO. Acceptable as a warning flag for Task 8 manual smoke.

timing_assumptions.assumption: partial_state persisted in Phase 3 is structurally compatible with the frontend CompositionState type at panel-open time
timing_assumptions.what_breaks_it: Backend deploy between compose-failure and panel-open

timing_assumptions.assumption: sendMessage and retryMessage catch blocks fire with a fully-populated ApiError (including partial_state) before any component re-renders trigger a selectSession reset
timing_assumptions.what_breaks_it: Not a realistic concern — React state updates are synchronous within the catch block before any navigation

observability_gaps.description: spec §8.3.2 OTel counter composer.audit.audit_grade_view_total fires for every recovery transcript fetch. Plan does not add any dashboard or on-call signal specific to recovery panel usage. If a user's session enters a pathological retry-loop, counter increments accumulate without alerting.
observability_gaps.plan_coverage: None — overview §Done condition 5 requires counter post-conditions across the property-test campaign, but Phase 4 adds no new counter assertion test for the frontend-triggered access pattern
observability_gaps.recommendation: Task 5 Step 1 tests should assert fetch-once semantics to bound the counter increment rate per panel open.

failure_modes: Session deleted between compose failure and panel open (404)
limit=500 pagination window misses the relevant assistant row
integration_point_stress.plan_coverage: Task 5 covers loading and error states. 404 case (session deleted) not explicitly tested.

failure_modes: executionStore is in a running validation when clearValidation fires — safe because executionStore.clearValidation is synchronous per existing usage patterns

python_version_ast_union_syntax.notes: ast.Assign | ast.AnnAssign syntax requires Python 3.10+. Project is Python 3.12/3.13 per overview.

new_npm_dependencies.notes: Plan explicitly states no new npm dependencies. useFocusTrap confirmed at src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts:17.

task_0_split_recommendation.rationale: Task 0 is a 4247-line Python file refactor. Tasks 1-9 are a multi-component TypeScript feature. Combining them in one PR forces reviewers to context-switch between languages and concerns. The plan is structured so Task 0 is independently committable (Step 7 has its own commit message). Splitting Phase 4A as a prerequisite PR would reduce review cognitive load.
task_0_split_recommendation.recommendation: Consider splitting Task 0 into a prerequisite PR (Phase 4A) that merges before Phase 4B begins. The plan already structures this as a distinct commit. Non-blocking: the plan author has good reasons to bundle (single-phase review history, one PR citation in the PR chain).

blocking_issues.title: recoveryError and recoveryStartedEditorRevision not added to session-switch reset sites
blocking_issues.evidence: sessionStore.ts lines 175, 211, 237, 547 — four explicit session-context reset sites; none updated in the plan
blocking_issues.required_fix: Task 3 Step 2 must add recoveryError and recoveryStartedEditorRevision to all four reset sites. Recommend a clearedRecoveryState() helper analogous to clearedGuidedState() (sessionStore.ts:89-99) to prevent future drift.

blocking_issues.title: editorRevision increment trigger is undefined — concurrent-edit guard has no mechanically enforced semantics
blocking_issues.evidence: No editorRevision field exists anywhere in src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/. Plan introduces it in Task 3 Step 2 but does not specify which user actions increment it.
blocking_issues.required_fix: The plan must enumerate what constitutes an 'editor revision change.' At minimum: (1) every local node/edge/source/output mutation in the inspector, or (2) every server-confirmed compositionState update. The answer changes whether the guard catches local-only edits. Task 2 Step 1 tests must assert the guard fires for local edits made after the compose request was sent.

warnings: W-SOE-3: RecoveryTranscript re-render guard — Task 5 must specify useEffect fetch-once semantics to prevent multiple audit_access_log writes per panel open (spec §8.3.2 composer.audit.audit_grade_view_total counter)
W-SOE-4: Retry-after-failure second panel open — Task 3 Step 1 should add a test that second failure replaces first recoveryError; Task 3 Step 2 should document replacement semantics

W-SOE-5: z-index stacking unspecified — Task 6 Step 2 should explicitly assign --z-dialog (201) to recovery panel to match existing modal layer; verify simultaneous fanout-guard + recovery-panel scenario in Task 8 smoke
W-SOE-6: applyRecoveredState server-sync contract undefined — clarify whether Apply is local-only or triggers server state promotion
W-SOE-7: No schema-version guard on partial_state deserialization — document as accepted gap or add a runtime shape check before installing partial_state

W-INTEGRATION: Integration eval characterization test tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py:262 calls _run_one_turn_for_test but is NOT in Task 0 Step 5 focused verification run — include it or note explicitly why it is excluded

W-PAGINATION: limit=500 for transcript fetch may not include the relevant failed-turn assistant row if session has >500 combined rows; Task 5 Step 1 degraded-path test should cover assistant_message_id present but not found in fetch window
recommendations: R-1: Extract clearedRecoveryState() helper (analogous to clearedGuidedState()) and compose into all four session-switch reset sites at once — prevents future field drift
R-2: Consider splitting Task 0 (Phase 4A) into a prerequisite PR to reduce reviewer cognitive load across languages
R-3: Task 8 Step 2 manual smoke checklist should include: (a) simultaneous fanout-guard dialog + recovery panel visible, (b) session switch while panel is open clears the panel on the new session
